rulings.Case Title: MAMANDUR FAMILY TRUST VS. THE POLARIS GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication ******Re-Noticed*******
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 12, 2025, line 2. DEFENDANTS SHALINI SADDA, DAVID COHEN, CITY REAL ESTATE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/adjudication is DENIED. Moving party fails to comply with CCP 1010 and CRC 3.1350(b) ("If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts."). Cases cited in the reply for the proposition that the defective notice for motion for summary judgment should be disregarded are inapposite. None of the cited cases found that the defective notice could be disregarded. None of the cited cases suggest that a single mention of "summary judgment on all of the claims" in the "Conclusion" section of the brief saves the defective notice. The Court notes that Summary Judgment is not even mentioned in the "Argument" section of the brief, which consists of subsections dedicated solely to individual causes of action of which summary adjudication is requested (albeit also improperly as issues listed in the Notice of Motion do not match the issues listed in the Separate Statement). =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRETT STEWART VS. ANDRE R. PATTERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 12, 2025, line 13. DEFENDANT ANDRE PATTERSON'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Court notes that moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1110, 3.1112 and CCP 1170. No code-compliant grounds for demurrer presented in the purported Points and Authorities. The function of a demurrer is to test the sufficiency of a pleading by raising questions of law. In assessing the sufficiency of a demurrer, all facts pleaded in the complaint must be deemed true. This motion appears to raise affirmative defenses, which is not the proper use of a demurrer. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
